title: CSS for Accessibility Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties and tools for creating an inclusive and accessible web experience.

questions.question: What is the primary role of 'Color Contrast' in web accessibility?
options: To make the website load much faster.
To ensure that there is enough visual difference between text and its background to be easily readable.
To define the website's visual theme and fonts.
To hide images from certain types of users.

questions.question: Keyboard users rely on the ________ to know which element they are currently interacting with.
options: Click sound
Focus outline / Ring
Page title
Browser's address bar

questions.question: Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to hide text on the screen but still make it accessible to screen reader users?
options: display: none;
visibility: hidden;
Using a special '.sr-only' utility class.
Using absolute positioning with 'top: -9999px'.

questions.question: What does the '@media (prefers-reduced-motion)' query allow you to do for accessibility?
options: To automatically rotate the page by 45 degrees.
To detect if a user has requested a simplified experience with less animation (for vestibular disorders).
To choose the colors for the animation.
To automatically stop the animation halfway.

questions.question: Why is it important to never use 'outline: none;' without providing a visible replacement focus style?
options: It makes the site load much slower.
It makes navigation impossible for users who only use a keyboard.
It is not supported in modern browsers.
It hides all images.
